application_details.business_name: Thompson Building & Property Consulting Pty Ltd

qualifications__experience.formal_qualifications_field_of_expertise:
Bachelor of Construction Management (Honours), University of Western Sydney, 2005.
Diploma of Building and Construction (Building), TAFE NSW, 2001.
Certificate IV in Building and Construction, TAFE NSW, 1999.
Accredited Building Consultant (Residential and Low-Rise Commercial).
Accredited Timber Pest Inspector (for pre-purchase inspections).

Primary fields of expertise:
- Residential building inspections (pre-purchase, pre-settlement, handover, and defect reports) for detached houses, townhouses, and low-rise apartment buildings primarily across Greater Sydney and regional NSW.
- Dilapidation surveys and condition reports for neighbouring properties prior to and following major construction works, with a particular focus on documenting pre-existing cracking, movement, and moisture ingress in older brick veneer and double-brick dwellings.
- Building defect diagnosis and reporting, including waterproofing failures (balconies, bathrooms, and planter boxes), structural movement (subsidence, soil reactivity, and inadequate footing systems), and building envelope performance (roof leaks, flashing failures, cavity brick issues, and inadequate weep hole/drainage provisions).
- Expert witness work in NCAT and Local Court matters relating to residential building disputes, including preparation of Scott Schedules, joint expert reports, and giving oral evidence.
- Contract administration and quality assurance for small to medium residential projects, including extensions, major renovations, and new builds, with an emphasis on compliance with the NCC (BCA), relevant Australian Standards (e.g. AS 2870, AS 1684, AS 3959, AS 3740), and the NSW Guide to Standards and Tolerances.
- Strata and sinking fund forecast inspections for small to medium strata schemes, including common property condition assessments and long-term maintenance planning.

In practice, most of my week is split roughly as follows: two to three days conducting on-site inspections and taking photographs/measurements, one day preparing detailed written reports for clients (including annotated photos and recommendations), and one day dealing with dispute resolution matters, expert witness briefs, and liaising with solicitors, strata managers, and homeowners. I also regularly attend CPD seminars on waterproofing, fire safety, and changes to the NCC to maintain current knowledge and ensure my advice reflects best practice and current regulatory requirements.
qualifications__experience.association: Master Builders Association of New South Wales; Housing Industry Association (HIA)
qualifications__experience.category_of_membership: MBA NSW: Full Member (Contractor/Consultant). HIA: Technical/Professional Member.
